segments.text: Moreover Elihu answered,
Hear my words, you wise men. Give ear to me, you who have knowledge.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: For the ear tries words, as the palate tastes food.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Let us choose for us that which is right. Let us know among ourselves what is good.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: For Job has said, ‘I am righteous, God has taken away my right.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Notwithstanding my right I am considered a liar. My wound is incurable, though I am without disobedience.’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: What man is like Job, who drinks scorn like water,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: who goes in company with the workers of iniquity, and walks with wicked men?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For he has said, ‘It profits a man nothingthat he should delight himself with God.’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
“Therefore listen to me, you men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness, 
from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: For the work of a man he will give to him, and cause every man to find according to his ways.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Yes surely, God will not do wickedly, neither will the Almighty pervert justice.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Who put him in charge of the earth? Or who has appointed him over the whole world?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If he set his heart on himself, if he gathered to himself his spirit and his breath,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: all flesh would perish together, and man would turn again to dust.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “If now you have understanding, hear this. Listen to the voice of my words.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Should even one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn him who is righteous and mighty,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: who says to a king, ‘Vile!’or to nobles, ‘Wicked!’?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text:
He doesn’t respect the persons of princes, nor respect the rich more than the poor, 
for they all are the work of his hands.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
In a moment they die, even at midnight. The people are shaken and pass away. 
The mighty are taken away without a hand.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “For his eyes are on the ways of a man. He sees all his goings.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: There is no darkness, nor thick gloom, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For he doesn’t need to consider a man further, that he should go before God in judgment.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He breaks mighty men in pieces in ways past finding out, and sets others in their place.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore he takes knowledge of their works. He overturns them in the night, so that they are destroyed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He strikes them as wicked menin the open sight of others;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: because they turned away from following him, and wouldn’t pay attention to any of his ways,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: so that they caused the cry of the poor to come to him. He heard the cry of the afflicted.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
When he gives quietness, who then can condemn? When he hides his face, who then can see him?
He is over a nation or a man alike,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: that the godless man may not reign, that there be no one to ensnare the people.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “For has any said to God,‘I am guilty, but I will not offend any more.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Teach me that which I don’t see. If I have done iniquity, I will do it no more’?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Shall his recompense be as you desire, that you refuse it? For you must choose, and not I. 
Therefore speak what you know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Men of understanding will tell me, yes, every wise man who hears me:
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: ‘Job speaks without knowledge. His words are without wisdom.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I wish that Job were tried to the end, because of his answering like wicked men.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
For he adds rebellion to his sin. He claps his hands among us, 
and multiplies his words against God.
segments.speaker: Unknown
